rows.notes: Large centered year at top of page, very clear and unambiguous.
Centered title directly beneath year. Text is legible and clearly a section header.

rows.description: 10 per cent on Estates, Supp Div'd 1880
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount aligned with the first of the three ruling columns; pounds '66' and shillings '13' reasonably clear. Pence looks like '6' with a small halfpenny mark (interpreted as 0.5). Slight ink unevenness on pence so confidence moderate.
Abbreviated description 'Do Do' (ditto). Pounds '373' and shillings '8' are aligned and readable. Pence appears as '6' with a tiny halfpenny loop; the halfpenny is faint so confidence is moderate.
Description clear. Pounds and shillings columns align with previous rows. Pence reads '6' with a small halfpenny mark similar to above lines; ink slightly faded on fraction sign.
Short description 'Bushey Div'd' legible. Pounds '7', shillings '4' clear. Pence '6' with faint halfpenny loop — consistent with preceding rows; moderate confidence due to small ink.
This line introduces a grouped set of numbered sub-entries below; no amounts in the three right columns. Header text is reasonably clear.

Sub-item under 'Masters Composition' labelled '1'. Amount in columns reads £22 3s 0d clearly. There are scribbled notes in the description area (rates etc.) but the three-column amount is aligned and legible.
Sub-item '2' under same header. Pounds '11' and shillings '8' read clearly. Pence looks like '6' with a halfpenny loop; the fraction mark is faint making the pence reading moderately uncertain.
Line labelled '3' followed by 'Nil' (appears to be explicitly 'nil'). No amounts in the three columns. Description and lack of amounts are clear.
Description legible. Amount aligns to show £3 0s 0d. Straightforward and clear.
Text 'Dickenson. Balance of Rent' clear. Amount reads £43 0s 0d in the pounds/shillings/pence columns without ambiguity.

Interest line: the small amount '1 14 4½' is written in the three columns and aligns under the totals; shillings '14' and pence '4' are readable and a small halfpenny loop indicates 0.5. Some ink faintness on the fraction but alignment supports this reading.
Description clear. Amount reads £2 10s 0d, nicely aligned; digits have good contrast.

rows.description: Int on £100 at 4 per cent Augt 18-1880 by Do
rows.notes: Interest on £100. Amount in columns clearly '£4 0s 0d'. Handwriting in description slightly cramped but numeric columns are unambiguous.

rows.description: Int on £150 at Do to April 1.1880 by Do
rows.notes: Another interest line. Pounds '6' and zero shillings/pence are clear in the columns.

Description is slightly unclear (reads 'late Tutors Prize' or similar). Amount appears as '13 2 10½' in the columns; the halfpenny sign is faint. Because the description is uncertain, confidence is moderate.

rows.description: Int for one yr to Domus for £500 by Bursar
rows.notes: Text lengthy but legible. Amount reads clearly as £15 0s 0d in the aligned columns.
Description looks truncated (ends 'of'); aligned amount reads '£114 4s 3d'. The ones column digits are slightly cramped causing moderate confidence.
Handwritten description likely 'Bouse Register' or similar; amount '£10 0s 0d' is well aligned. Description spelling uncertain but numeric columns clear.
Very clear bank cash entry with printed-like numbers in the amount columns: '122 15 0'. Alignment with ruling lines is unambiguous.
'Do Do Richards' (ditto) is clear. Amount '£93 3s 4d' is crisply written and aligns correctly with the columns.
Third 'Do Do' line with name that reads 'Tuckell' (or similar). Amount '£46 19s 8d' is clear in the three-column alignment; letters in the name are a bit flourishy but readable.

rows.description: Stock of the three funds sold 10607:17:4
rows.notes: The descriptive text contains a numeric-looking figure ('10607:17:4' or similar) written as part of the description rather than in the right-hand £ s d columns. No aligned amount appears in the three ruling columns for this line; because the numeric is embedded in description and partly faded, confidence is moderate.

Description 'Canal Shares Sold' clear. Amount reads '£347 4s 0d' in the three columns. To the far right there is another grouped figure (see next row) which appears to be a running subtotal rather than this particular line's column amount.

A larger right-hand figure '2277 19 4' appears aligned vertically to the right of the Canal Shares and the preceding fund/stock lines — interpreted as a subtotal/combined amount. The number is clear but its relation to which exact lines are subtotalled is slightly ambiguous, so classed as a 'total' with moderate confidence.
Description partially unclear (reads 'Sejbones' or 'Sejborne' College) but the beneficiary 'for William Road' is legible. Amount '£10 0s 0d' is clear in the columns.
Line reads 'Hammond. Bursar allowed to Soden' (surname on right may be 'Soden' or 'Soden' variant). Amount '£25 0s 0d' clear in numeric columns; some uncertainty in the final word of description.
Possibly 'Baase Register' or 'Bause Register' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount reads '£2 0s 0d' clearly. Description spelling uncertain; numeric column is clear.
Description appears to read 'Clutter book for William Road' (could be 'Cluster' or 'Clutter'). Amount is '£10 0s 0d' clearly shown in the amount columns.

Final bottom-right grand total reads '3390 0 4½' (£3390 0s 4½d). The large pounds figure and the 4 with a clear halfpenny loop are visible; slight smudge on the last digit reduces confidence to moderate-high.
